003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.logging;
018
019import static java.util.logging.Level.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.*;
024import java.util.logging.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
027
028/**
029 * Extended logger that provides convenience methods for logging formatted messages.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * This class extends {@link java.util.logging.Logger} and uses delegation internally to wrap
033 * an underlying {@link java.util.logging.Logger} instance. This design provides:
034 * </p>
035 * <ul>
036 *    <li>Type compatibility - extends Logger for use anywhere a Logger is expected
037 *    <li>Flexibility - can wrap existing logger instances
038 *    <li>Convenience methods for formatted logging using {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils#fs(String, Object...)}
039 * </ul>
040 *
041 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
042 * <ul>
043 *    <li>All standard {@link java.util.logging.Logger} functionality via delegation
044 *    <li>Convenience methods for formatted logging at all log levels
045 *    <li>Lazy evaluation of formatted messages using {@link Utils#fs(String, Object...)}
046 *    <li>Support for both MessageFormat-style and printf-style formatting
047 * </ul>
048 *
049 * <h5 class='section'>Format Support:</h5>
050 * <p>
051 * The formatting methods support both MessageFormat-style and printf-style formatting:
052 * </p>
053 * <ul>
054 *    <li><b>Printf-style:</b> <js>"%s"</js>, <js>"%d"</js>, <js>"%.2f"</js>, etc.
055 *    <li><b>MessageFormat-style:</b> <js>"{0}"</js>, <js>"{1,number}"</js>, etc.
056 *    <li><b>Un-numbered MessageFormat:</b> <js>"{}"</js> - Sequential placeholders
057 * </ul>
058 *
059 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
060 * <p class='bjava'>
061 *    Logger <jv>logger</jv> = Logger.getLogger(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getName());
062 *
063 *    <jc>// Formatted logging at different levels</jc>
064 *    <jv>logger</jv>.severe(<js>"Error processing user {0}: {1}"</js>, userId, error);
065 *    <jv>logger</jv>.warning(<js>"Failed to connect to %s after %d attempts"</js>, hostname, attempts);
066 *    <jv>logger</jv>.info(<js>"Processing {} items"</js>, count);
067 *    <jv>logger</jv>.fine(<js>"Debug value: %s"</js>, debugValue);
068 * </p>
069 *
070 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
071 *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils#fs(String, Object...)}
072 *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils#f(String, Object...)}
073 *    <li class='jc'>{@link java.util.logging.Logger}
074 * </ul>
075 */
076@SuppressWarnings({
077   "java:S100",  // Method names match java.util.logging.Logger for API compatibility
078   "java:S1192", // String literals intentionally duplicated for clarity
079   "java:S2176"  // Class name intentionally matches java.util.logging.Logger; extends it to provide Juneau-specific logging enhancements
080})
081public class Logger extends java.util.logging.Logger {
082
083   /**
084    * Permanent registry of logger instances by name.
085    *
086    * <p>
087    * Uses an unbounded {@link ConcurrentHashMap} so every name maps to exactly one
088    * {@link Logger} instance for the JVM lifetime.  An evictable cache (e.g. one with a
089    * {@code maxSize} that clears the whole map) would cause a class-loaded
090    * {@code static final Logger} field to refer to an instance that is no longer in the
091    * registry; a subsequent call to {@link #getLogger(String)} would then return a new,
092    * different instance, breaking {@link LogRecordCapture} and any other code that
093    * assumes one name == one identity.
094    */
095   private static final ConcurrentHashMap<String, Logger> loggers = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
096
097   /**
098    * The underlying logger instance that we delegate to.
099    */
100   private final java.util.logging.Logger delegate;
101
102   /**
103    * List of log record listeners.
104    */
105   private final List<LogRecordListener> listeners = Collections.synchronizedList(new ArrayList<>());
106
107   /**
108    * Protected constructor - wraps an existing logger instance.
109    *
110    * @param delegate The underlying logger to delegate to.
111    */
112   protected Logger(java.util.logging.Logger delegate) {
113      super(delegate.getName(), null);
114      this.delegate = delegate;
115   }
116
117   /**
118    * Creates a logger for the specified name.
119    *
120    * <p>
121    * This method returns the same Logger instance for a given name, ensuring that
122    * listeners attached to a logger persist across multiple calls to {@link #getLogger(String)}.
123    * Logger instances are cached and automatically created using the underlying
124    * {@link java.util.logging.Logger#getLogger(String)}.
125    *
126    * @param name The logger name.
127    * @return A logger instance (cached and reused for the same name).
128    */
129   public static Logger getLogger(String name) {
130      return loggers.computeIfAbsent(name, k -> new Logger(java.util.logging.Logger.getLogger(k)));
131   }
132
133   /**
134    * Creates a logger for the specified class.
135    *
136    * @param clazz The class.
137    * @return A logger instance.
138    */
139   public static Logger getLogger(Class<?> clazz) {
140      return getLogger(cn(clazz));
141   }
142
143   /**
144    * Creates a log record capture for testing.
145    *
146    * <p>
147    * This method creates a {@link LogRecordCapture} that will capture all log records
148    * from this logger. The capture should be closed when done (typically using try-with-resources).
149    *
150    * @return A LogRecordCapture instance.
151    */
152   @SuppressWarnings({
153      "resource" // Caller takes ownership of the returned LogRecordCapture
154   })
155   public LogRecordCapture captureEvents() {
156      return new LogRecordCapture(this);
157   }
158
159   /**
160    * Adds a log record listener.
161    *
162    * @param listener The listener to add.
163    */
164   void addLogRecordListener(LogRecordListener listener) {
165      listeners.add(listener);
166   }
167
168   /**
169    * Removes a log record listener.
170    *
171    * @param listener The listener to remove.
172    */
173   void removeLogRecordListener(LogRecordListener listener) {
174      listeners.remove(listener);
175   }
176
177   /**
178    * Central logging method that all logging calls feed through.
179    *
180    * <p>
181    * This method creates a {@link LogRecord}, notifies all listeners, and then delegates
182    * to the underlying logger.
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * The method will proceed even if the log level is not loggable if there are listeners
186    * attached, allowing listeners to capture all log records regardless of level.
187    *
188    * @param level The log level.
189    * @param msg The log message or format pattern.
190    * @param args The format arguments, or <jk>null</jk> if none.
191    * @param thrown The throwable, or <jk>null</jk> if none.
192    */
193   private void doLog(Level level, String msg, Object[] args, Throwable thrown) {
194      if (!isLoggable(level) && listeners.isEmpty())
195         return;
196
197      // Create LogRecord with lazy formatting support
198      var rec = new LogRecord(getName(), level, msg, args, thrown);
199
200      // Notify all listeners
201      listeners.forEach(x -> x.onLogRecord(rec));
202
203      // Delegate to underlying logger (LogRecord extends java.util.logging.LogRecord)
204      delegate.log(rec);
205   }
206
207   // Convenience methods with formatted strings
208
209   // Standard Logger methods - feed through central doLog method
210   @Override
211   public void severe(String msg) {
212      doLog(SEVERE, msg, null, null);
213   }
214
215   @Override
216   public void warning(String msg) {
217      doLog(WARNING, msg, null, null);
218   }
219
220   @Override
221   public void info(String msg) {
222      doLog(INFO, msg, null, null);
223   }
224
225   @Override
226   public void config(String msg) {
227      doLog(CONFIG, msg, null, null);
228   }
229
230   @Override
231   public void fine(String msg) {
232      doLog(FINE, msg, null, null);
233   }
234
235   @Override
236   public void finer(String msg) {
237      doLog(FINER, msg, null, null);
238   }
239
240   @Override
241   public void finest(String msg) {
242      doLog(FINEST, msg, null, null);
243   }
244
245   @Override
246   public void log(Level level, String msg) {
247      doLog(level, msg, null, null);
248   }
249
250   @Override
251   public void log(Level level, String msg, Object param1) {
252      doLog(level, msg, new Object[]{param1}, null);
253   }
254
255   @Override
256   public void log(Level level, String msg, Object[] params) {
257      doLog(level, msg, params, null);
258   }
259
260   @Override
261   public void log(Level level, String msg, Throwable thrown) {
262      doLog(level, msg, null, thrown);
263   }
264
265   @Override
266   public boolean isLoggable(Level level) {
267      return delegate.isLoggable(level);
268   }
269
270   @Override
271   public Level getLevel() {
272      return delegate.getLevel();
273   }
274
275   @Override
276   public void setLevel(Level newLevel) {
277      delegate.setLevel(newLevel);
278   }
279
280   @Override
281   public String getName() {
282      return delegate.getName();
283   }
284
285   @Override
286   public void addHandler(Handler handler) {
287      delegate.addHandler(handler);
288   }
289
290   @Override
291   public void removeHandler(Handler handler) {
292      delegate.removeHandler(handler);
293   }
294
295   @Override
296   public Handler[] getHandlers() {
297      return delegate.getHandlers();
298   }
299
300   // Convenience methods with formatted strings
301
302   /**
303    * Logs a SEVERE level message with formatted string.
304    *
305    * @param pattern The format pattern.
306    * @param args The format arguments.
307    */
308   public void severe(String pattern, Object...args) {
309      doLog(SEVERE, pattern, args, null);
310   }
311
312   /**
313    * Logs a SEVERE level message with formatted string and throwable.
314    *
315    * @param thrown The throwable.
316    * @param pattern The format pattern.
317    * @param args The format arguments.
318    */
319   public void severe(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
320      doLog(SEVERE, pattern, args, thrown);
321   }
322
323   /**
324    * Logs a WARNING level message with formatted string.
325    *
326    * @param pattern The format pattern.
327    * @param args The format arguments.
328    */
329   public void warning(String pattern, Object...args) {
330      doLog(WARNING, pattern, args, null);
331   }
332
333   /**
334    * Logs a WARNING level message with formatted string and throwable.
335    *
336    * @param thrown The throwable.
337    * @param pattern The format pattern.
338    * @param args The format arguments.
339    */
340   public void warning(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
341      doLog(WARNING, pattern, args, thrown);
342   }
343
344   /**
345    * Logs an INFO level message with formatted string.
346    *
347    * @param pattern The format pattern.
348    * @param args The format arguments.
349    */
350   public void info(String pattern, Object...args) {
351      doLog(INFO, pattern, args, null);
352   }
353
354   /**
355    * Logs an INFO level message with formatted string and throwable.
356    *
357    * @param thrown The throwable.
358    * @param pattern The format pattern.
359    * @param args The format arguments.
360    */
361   public void info(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
362      doLog(INFO, pattern, args, thrown);
363   }
364
365   /**
366    * Logs a CONFIG level message with formatted string.
367    *
368    * @param pattern The format pattern.
369    * @param args The format arguments.
370    */
371   public void config(String pattern, Object...args) {
372      doLog(CONFIG, pattern, args, null);
373   }
374
375   /**
376    * Logs a CONFIG level message with formatted string and throwable.
377    *
378    * @param thrown The throwable.
379    * @param pattern The format pattern.
380    * @param args The format arguments.
381    */
382   public void config(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
383      doLog(CONFIG, pattern, args, thrown);
384   }
385
386   /**
387    * Logs a FINE level message with formatted string.
388    *
389    * @param pattern The format pattern.
390    * @param args The format arguments.
391    */
392   public void fine(String pattern, Object...args) {
393      doLog(FINE, pattern, args, null);
394   }
395
396   /**
397    * Logs a FINE level message with formatted string and throwable.
398    *
399    * @param thrown The throwable.
400    * @param pattern The format pattern.
401    * @param args The format arguments.
402    */
403   public void fine(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
404      doLog(FINE, pattern, args, thrown);
405   }
406
407   /**
408    * Logs a FINER level message with formatted string.
409    *
410    * @param pattern The format pattern.
411    * @param args The format arguments.
412    */
413   public void finer(String pattern, Object...args) {
414      doLog(FINER, pattern, args, null);
415   }
416
417   /**
418    * Logs a FINER level message with formatted string and throwable.
419    *
420    * @param thrown The throwable.
421    * @param pattern The format pattern.
422    * @param args The format arguments.
423    */
424   public void finer(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
425      doLog(FINER, pattern, args, thrown);
426   }
427
428   /**
429    * Logs a FINEST level message with formatted string.
430    *
431    * @param pattern The format pattern.
432    * @param args The format arguments.
433    */
434   public void finest(String pattern, Object...args) {
435      doLog(FINEST, pattern, args, null);
436   }
437
438   /**
439    * Logs a FINEST level message with formatted string and throwable.
440    *
441    * @param thrown The throwable.
442    * @param pattern The format pattern.
443    * @param args The format arguments.
444    */
445   public void finest(Throwable thrown, String pattern, Object...args) {
446      doLog(FINEST, pattern, args, thrown);
447   }
448
449   /**
450    * Logs a message at the specified level with formatted string.
451    *
452    * @param level The log level.
453    * @param pattern The format pattern.
454    * @param args The format arguments.
455    */
456   public void logf(Level level, String pattern, Object...args) {
457      doLog(level, pattern, args, null);
458   }
459
460   /**
461    * Logs a message at the specified level with formatted string and throwable.
462    *
463    * @param level The log level.
464    * @param pattern The format pattern.
465    * @param thrown The throwable.
466    * @param args The format arguments.
467    */
468   public void logf(Level level, String pattern, Throwable thrown, Object...args) {
469      doLog(level, pattern, args, thrown);
470   }
471}
